rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee clauses in valid component type or implementation scopes based on whether they reference ports or subcomponents.
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for conditional and temporal expressions.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current component scope.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must use valid syntax: assume "label": expr; guarantee "label": expr; eq name : type = expr; const name : type = value; assign output = expr;
Component type contracts should use visible feature names and constants, not subcomponent ports directly.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables unless explicitly typed in the model.
Do not invent new identifiers; always use visible symbols from the current component scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints not tied to visible ports or constants
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
